dashboard.title: FIRE LEDGER
dashboard.chartSubtitle: Growth including compound interest and statutory pension

planner.title: FINANCE CHECK
planner.monthlySavingsHint: Amount you invest or save each month.
planner.savingsGrowthRateHint: Annual percentage increase of your savings rate. E.g. 3% if you expect salary increases.
planner.pensionMonthlyHint: Estimated statutory pension. Check your current pension statement.
planner.taxEtf: Used for wealth projection. Set your actual withdrawal rate below.
planner.etfWithdrawalRateHint: The percentage of your ETF portfolio you withdraw annually. Classic: 4% (Trinity Study). Used independently of the return rate for FIRE calculations.
planner.taxCash: Only interest income is considered for cash — no principal withdrawal. €1,000 allowance per person.
planner.retirementBudgetSub: Your planned monthly expenses in retirement
planner.inflationRateHint: Annual cost increase in retirement. 0% = no adjustment. Typical: 2% (ECB target).
planner.assetTaxRateTitle: Flat tax rate on asset income
planner.assetTaxRateHint: Enter the tax rate applied to your asset income. For simplicity, this rate is applied uniformly across all asset types. Typical: 25% capital gains tax + 5.5% solidarity surcharge = 26.375%. Including church tax approx. 27.8–28.6%.
planner.architectureTitle: The architecture of your financial freedom.
planner.gkvCapped: The maximum GKV amount of {{amount}} € / month will be considered (contribution cap).
planner.gkvUncapped: Approximately 21% of your asset income in the FIRE year will be considered (~{{amount}} € / month, max. 1,300 €).
planner.foundationLaid: of your financial foundation is laid.

prognosis.title: FORECAST
prognosis.disclaimerText: This forecast is for guidance only. Calculations are adjusted for inflation (real values). Actual market volatility may cause deviations. Regularly review your assumptions in

monteCarlo.title: Monte Carlo Simulation
monteCarlo.subtitle: FINANCIAL SUSTAINABILITY ANALYSIS
monteCarlo.successInfo: Your capital lasts until age 100 in {{count}} out of {{total}} simulated market cycles.
monteCarlo.simulationStart: The simulation starts with {{capital}} portfolio capital in the year {{year}} – based on your configuration.
monteCarlo.pessimisticDesc: The most unfavorable scenario with high inflation and low market growth.
monteCarlo.medianInheritanceDesc: Expected asset surplus at the end of the simulation period.
monteCarlo.chartHint: Tap on the chart for details
monteCarlo.volatilityInfo: Here you can adjust the volatility of market returns per year. Higher volatility leads to larger fluctuations in wealth development, which increases both opportunities and risks.
monteCarlo.expensesInfo: Here you must account for all costs: variable expenses, fixed costs (such as health insurance) and capital gains tax.

tax.title: FIRE LEDGER
tax.fireCalculationSub: The standard without special conditions – your baseline forecast.
tax.hardcoreFireSub: What if I reduce my variable expenses by 50%?
tax.partTimeSub: What if I work one day less from now on? (Reduce savings rate by 30%)
tax.partTimeHint: +1 free day per week from now.
tax.crashSub: Will my plan withstand a crash next year?
tax.monteCarloSub: How likely is it that my capital will last until age 100?
tax.monteCarloHint: Stochastic analysis with inflation range & volatility.
